chapterTitle: Contract Fulfilled in Letter, Broken in Spirit
summary: Chapter 20 revision PASSED adversarial gate with 2 warnings. 2 critical findings verified addressed: water-domain voice markers reduced from ~25 to ~12 instances (~52%, exceeding 40% target; LINE-001), classification/operative vocabulary reduced from 27 grep-matching lines to 19 (~30% by lines; individual marker tokens reduced further per stacking removal; LINE-002). 6 major findings verified: aftermath scene expanded +165 words with high-quality breathing-room material (DEV-201), 'through the bond' exact phrase eliminated 8→0 with varied constructions (LINE-003), all 5 clichés eliminated bringing count to 0 (LINE-004), 4 of 5 emotion-label clusters converted to sensation-based showing with 1 editor-exempted retention (LINE-005), anaphoric 'Not X' pattern reduced from 6 flagged instances to 2 retained strongest — but see WARN-001 (LINE-006), purple prose trimmed from 3→2 antithetical pairs and 3→2 adjectives (LINE-007). 1 moderate finding verified: 'god-seal dissolution' corrected to avoid implying the god-seal itself is dissolved (COPY-001). All 17 minor findings verified addressed with specific textual evidence. 1 minor finding deferred with valid rationale (COPY-005 British English — manuscript convention confirmed). 2 warnings issued: (1) 'In the bond' repetition pattern in aftermath scene (4 instances in 30 lines), partially undermining LINE-003's spirit; (2) anaphoric 'Not X. Not Y.' pattern at 4 instances rather than claimed 2 — L217 was claimed restructured but remains unchanged, L237 introduces a new instance. Both warnings are below the 3-warning failure threshold. Zero regressions in voice, continuity, pacing, show-don't-tell, or dialogue naturalism. Zero darlings survived. Traceability complete — all changes attributed to finding IDs. Word count +7.5% (4055→4434 prose words), justified by aftermath expansion and added specificity. Chapter proceeds to beta reading.

wordCountAnalysis.assessment: Word count increased +9.3% (wc -w including frontmatter YAML; frontmatter claims 4332 prose). Increase is fully justified: aftermath scene expansion (+~165 words per DEV-201), binding resistance specificity (+~45 words per DEV-203), Seep emotional registration beat (+~60 words per DEV-204), Kaelith death experience beat (+~35 words per DEV-202), and net additions from cliché replacements and show-don't-tell conversions that are more specific than their originals. No unjustified padding detected. Well within the 10% warning and 20% auto-fail thresholds.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: Voice marker saturation — water/current metaphor domain

preservedAtPeaks: L153: 'The current that had run between them for weeks — constant, living, the pulse of Kaelith's presence' — bond-silence scene, strongest instance, correctly retained
L165: 'like a current forcing its way through a frozen channel' — resuscitation reach, second strongest, correctly retained
L183: 'surfacing from deep water' — bond return, third peak, correctly retained
L227: 'Surface and depth' — thematic resolution, correctly retained
L229: 'surface tension gives way' — most original metaphor, correctly retained
eliminatedExamples: Draft L18 'current mapping a new channel' → Revised L49 'tracing with the patience of something that knew exactly where it was going' — literal replacement
Draft L22 'deep as an undertow' → Revised L53 'deeper than she could afford to examine' — reduced to adverb, no water metaphor
Draft L22 'trust like a current she could not redirect' → Revised L53 'trust she had not earned and could not set aside' — plain language
Draft L30 'wide enough to drown in' → Revised L61 'too wide to hold' — water imagery removed
Draft L50 'the current of what Kaelith was not saying' → Revised L81 'what Kaelith was not saying' — stripped to bare statement
Draft L56 'undertow of her awareness' → Revised L87 'baseline of her awareness' — analytical domain replacement
Draft L66 'current that has found its channel' → Revised L97 'a steadiness in the shared resonance that did not waver' — sensation-based replacement
Draft L116 'like a wave breaking' → Revised L147 'with a force that buckled her knees' — physical action replacement
Draft L124 'the cold current of distrust' → Revised L155 'the stiff cold of distrust' — temperature retained, current removed
Draft L144 'like a tide she could not stop tracking' → Revised L175 'listening to it, measuring it, holding it' — analytical domain
Draft L176 'absence of a current... beneath the surface' → Revised L207 'absence of a weight... pressing along the spine' — tactile replacement
Draft L200 'no undertow of compulsion' → Revised L231 'no residual pull' — generic physical term

Draft L202 'the steady current of two people... flood of the resuscitation' → Revised L233 'the quiet certainty of two people... crashing overwhelm of the resuscitation' — current and flood both removed
evidence.residualDensity: ~12 water-domain markers across 259 lines = ~1 per 22 lines. Execution scene has highest density (~7-8 markers in 80 lines) concentrated at the three emotional peaks. Preparation scene has 2 markers in 72 lines. Aftermath scene has 2 markers in 62 lines. No paragraph contains stacked water metaphors. Substantial improvement from draft's ~1 per 9 lines.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: Voice marker saturation — classification/operative vocabulary domain

evidence.note: Grep line reduction understates actual token reduction because draft lines frequently contained 2-3 stacked markers (the core problem). Specific token changes verified below.

precision_precise.note: Draft L58 'precision' → revised L89 'exactness'; draft L22 'operative precision' → revised L53 'meticulous calibration'; draft L30 'precise word' → revised: paused, selecting the — word removed; draft L68 'precise weight' → revised L99 'weight'; draft L198 'precise moment' → revised: moment (word removed); draft L210 'clipped, precise' → revised L247 'clipped, direct'

clinical.note: Draft L30 'clinical, flat' → revised L61 'flat, measured'; draft L88 'clinical framework' → revised L119 'Court's frameworks'; draft L186 'clinical thoroughness' → revised L217 'the thoroughness the Court had trained into her'; retained: L61 'clinical membrane', L105 'clinical register', L227 'clinical membrane'

operative.note: Draft L22 'operative precision' eliminated; draft L88 'operational protocols' → 'Seep's protocols'; draft L186 'operative running a diagnostic' → eliminated; retained: L209 'operative checking', L225 'not the operative'

analytical.note: Draft L126 'Her analytical mind counted' → revised L157 'Her mind counted'; retained: L93 'The analytical framework held'

evidence.stackingReduction: Draft L30 had 'clinical' ×2 + 'parameters' + 'precise' in one paragraph → revised L61 has 'clinical' ×1 + 'parameters' ×1 (reduced from 4→2). Draft L88 had 'clinical framework' + 'operational protocols' + 'precision' in three consecutive clauses → revised L119 has none of these three terms. Draft L186 had 'clinical' + 'operative' + 'diagnostic' stacked → revised L217 has 'thoroughness' alone. Major stacking clusters eliminated.
evidence.contextualJustification: Preparation scene (L41-L113) retains highest classification density (~12 markers in 72 lines) because it IS the clinical planning scene — classification vocabulary is contextually appropriate here. Execution and aftermath scenes show substantial reduction.

variedConstructions: L81: 'carried in the resonance between them'
L193: 'The bond carried everything at once'
L237: 'The bond carried what it carried'
evidence.warningDetail: See WARN-001. 'In the bond' appears 4 times in the aftermath scene (L225, L229, L241, L255) within ~30 lines, each time followed by 'Lirien felt/could feel.' This creates a new formulaic access pattern that partially replicates the original monotony, albeit with different phrasing. The letter of the finding is satisfied (exact phrase eliminated, well under the 4-maximum), but the spirit is partially undermined.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: INV-017 violation: cliché count exceeds maximum

eliminatedInstances.original: Draft L58: 'freedom would taste like ash in the mouth'
eliminatedInstances.revised: Revised L89: 'freedom would carry the weight of a contract completed against its own purpose'
eliminatedInstances.assessment: Stock metaphor replaced with contractual-domain imagery specific to Lirien and this story. Effective.

eliminatedInstances.original: Draft L60: 'She held that thought like a torch against the dark'
eliminatedInstances.revised: Revised L91: 'She held that thought against the worst case the way she held a datum against a hypothesis'
eliminatedInstances.assessment: Stock courage image replaced with analytical-domain comparison. Character-appropriate.

eliminatedInstances.original: Draft L158: 'the most beautiful thing Lirien had ever heard'
eliminatedInstances.revised: Revised L189: Cut entirely — 'The sound — ragged, ugly, the desperate inhalation... — was *present*.'
eliminatedInstances.assessment: Stock superlative removed. Anti-beauty description carries emotional weight more effectively alone.

eliminatedInstances.original: Draft L162: 'Kaelith's heartbeat hammering back to life'
eliminatedInstances.revised: Revised L193: 'Kaelith's heartbeat reasserting itself in uneven, insistent rhythm'
eliminatedInstances.assessment: Near-banned 'heart hammering' replaced with specific physiological description.

eliminatedInstances.original: Draft L162: 'Kaelith's relief breaking over her in waves'
eliminatedInstances.revised: Revised L193: 'The grip of her fingers — refusing to let go'
eliminatedInstances.assessment: Stock emotional-wave metaphor replaced with physical action showing. Show-don't-tell improvement simultaneously.

evidence.grepVerification: grep for 'taste like ash', 'torch against the dark', 'most beautiful', 'hammering', 'breaking over.*waves' — all return zero matches in revised.

conversions.original: Draft L66: 'Kaelith's determination: steady, deep' + 'the matching terror'
conversions.revised: Revised L97: 'a steadiness in the shared resonance that did not waver, rooted and immovable' + 'a tension that pressed against Lirien's ribs'
conversions.assessment: Named emotions (determination, terror) replaced with physical sensations (steadiness, tension against ribs). Effective show-don't-tell.

conversions.original: Draft L124: 'grief... anger, betrayal, the cold current of distrust'
conversions.revised: Revised L155: 'grief... anger, betrayal, the stiff cold of distrust' — RETAINED per editor exemption
conversions.assessment: Correctly retained. Editor's note that the catalogue of past emotions contrasted with present absence is effective precisely because it names what was there to emphasise what is gone.

conversions.original: Draft L154: 'fear and relief and pain and a fierce, stunned gratitude'
conversions.revised: Revised L185: 'a pulse fighting to find its rhythm, lungs dragging at air, warmth flooding extremities that had gone cold — and underneath it all, a gratitude so sharp it hurt'
conversions.assessment: Four named emotions replaced with three physical sensations + one named emotion ('gratitude'). Substantial improvement. 'Gratitude' retained but qualified with sensory intensifier ('so sharp it hurt').

conversions.original: Draft L162: 'Kaelith's relief breaking over her in waves, Kaelith's terror'
conversions.revised: Revised L193: 'The grip of her fingers — refusing to let go. A shudder that ran through her whole body, arriving late, after the fact'
conversions.assessment: Named emotions replaced with physical actions (grip, shudder). Body's reckoning replaces terror label.

conversions.original: Draft L184: 'relief, exhaustion, a fierce and fragile tenderness'
conversions.revised: Revised L215: 'a loosening in the body, the heaviness of someone who has spent everything... a fierce and fragile tenderness'
conversions.assessment: Relief and exhaustion replaced with physical descriptors (loosening, heaviness). Tenderness retained — one named emotion in this cluster.

evidence.residualNamedEmotions: Outside the editor-exempted L155 passage: 'gratitude' at L185, 'tenderness' at L215. Two named emotions in the chapter's non-exempted body. Within the editor's recommended maximum of 2.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: Anaphoric 'Not X. Not Y. Not Z.' pattern overuse

instances.text: Not walled. Not muted. Not the guarded quiet...
instances.status: RETAINED — strongest instance, bond-silence scene. Correct per plan.

instances.text: Not Cerith Court precision. Not Seep methodology. Everything.
instances.status: RETAINED — second strongest, resuscitation scene. Correct per plan.

instances.text: Not because a contract had placed her in proximity. Not because a bond had tuned her to a specific heartbeat.
instances.status: NOT FIXED — revision log claims this was restructured, but comparison with draft L186 shows the 'Not because... Not because...' construction is preserved verbatim (only 'sensitised' → 'tuned' changed, which is a COPY-006 fix, not a LINE-006 restructure). TRACEABILITY CONCERN: claimed as addressed but change not executed.

instances.text: Not analysing. Not measuring. Just resting.
instances.status: NEW — introduced in the DEV-201 aftermath expansion. This is a new instance of the anaphoric pattern that the revision was supposed to be reducing.

eliminatedInstances: Draft L12 'Not dangerous-wrong. Not the thin, grey wrong...' → Revised L43 'neither the thin grey of a Pallor tear nor the too-bright edge' — ✓ restructured to neither/nor
Draft L100 'Not damage. Not destruction. Interruption.' → Revised L131 'A clean interruption, nothing more.' — ✓ collapsed to single statement
Draft L202 'Not the overwhelming flood...' → Revised L233 'Not the crashing overwhelm... but the quiet certainty' — ✓ restructured to single Not X but Y
evidence.assessment: 3 of 6 flagged instances successfully restructured. 2 correctly retained. 1 claimed as fixed but unchanged (L217). 1 new instance introduced (L237). Net: 4 instances remain vs. claimed 2. The L217 non-fix represents a minor traceability gap. The L237 addition is a regression from the revision itself. However, the 4 remaining instances use varied syntactic patterns (triple negation, double 'Not because', double gerund negation, single 'Not X but Y') reducing the structural monotony.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: Purple prose — triple antithesis with triple adjective stacking

evidence.original: Draft L132: 'not end but begin, not silence but sound, not death but the ragged, impossible, defiant continuation of a heartbeat that she refused to let remain stopped'
evidence.revised: Revised L163: 'not silence but sound, not death but the ragged, defiant continuation of a heartbeat she refused to let remain stopped'
changesVerified: Antithetical pair 'not end but begin' CUT — confirmed absent in revised. Least specific pair correctly identified and removed.
Adjective 'impossible' CUT — revised has 'ragged, defiant' (2 adjectives) vs. draft's 'ragged, impossible, defiant' (3). Within style guide's max-2 consecutive adjective limit.
evidence.assessment: Clean, minimal edit. Two pairs and two adjectives remain — within style guide limits. The retained pairs (silence/sound, death/continuation) are the strongest and most specific.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: Aftermath scene brevity relative to emotional significance

evidence.newMaterial: L237-241: Three new paragraphs between the tears beat (L235) and the wind-shift environmental transition (L243). Content: (1) 'Neither of them moved. Neither of them spoke.' — shared silence with bond carrying matched heartbeats; (2) 'She had never rested in another person before' — Lirien's first experience of undefended presence, every second a deliberate act of volition; (3) 'Kaelith's breathing was slow against her forehead' — quality of Kaelith's open stillness.
evidence.qualityAssessment: The new material is thematically resonant (volition, choice, absence of operative awareness), in Lirien's voice, and provides the breathing room the developmental editor requested. The emotional climax ('The binding is gone. You're still here.' at L223) now has 14 lines of uninterrupted processing before the Quietus redirect at L243. Draft had approximately 4 lines between proof-of-love and mission redirect. Substantial improvement.
evidence.transitionPreserved: The wind-shift/Pallor-smell environmental transition mechanism is retained unchanged at L243, arriving after the expanded quiet moment as the developmental editor recommended.

evidence.original: Draft L26: 'the god-seal dissolution at the Quietus still waited, but the binding could not'
evidence.revised: Revised L57: 'the Quietus still waited, but the binding could not'
evidence.assessment: Misleading 'god-seal dissolution' phrase removed entirely. The god-seal is reinforced (per ch21 events and revised L247: 'The god-seal still needs reinforcement'), not dissolved. Factual inconsistency eliminated.

evidence.original: Draft L160: 'Kaelith's eyes opened. Unfocused for a moment, then sharp — the clarity of a commander reassessing the battlefield.'
evidence.revised: Revised L191: 'Kaelith's eyes opened. Unfocused for a moment, then sharp — the clarity of a commander reassessing after engagement. But underneath the focus, something Lirien had not seen before: a brief, unplaceable expression, as if Kaelith had looked at something in the silence and found no framework for it. The expression lasted a heartbeat. Then her jaw set, and she was a soldier again.'
evidence.assessment: New beat acknowledges Kaelith experienced something during the eight-second death without breaking POV discipline. 'Unplaceable expression' + 'found no framework for it' is appropriately mysterious. Kaelith's rapid return to soldier-self is in character.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: Binding resistance mechanism could be more specific

evidence.original: Draft L104: 'its architecture pushed back against the loophole with a force that made her vision blur and her hands shake against Kaelith's chest.'
evidence.revised: Revised L135: 'its architecture pushed back — trying to redirect her magic from interruption to destruction, turning the scalpel into a hammer. Lirien's vision blurred. Her hands shook against Kaelith's chest. The binding wanted force where she needed finesse, and the pressure to comply made her fingers ache with the effort of holding the scalpel steady.'
evidence.assessment: Binding's counter-action now specific: redirecting precision to force (scalpel→hammer). Crisis (push through or abort) more concrete — Lirien's triumph is maintaining precision under compulsion. Scalpel/hammer metaphor consistent with earlier surgical framing.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: Seep tacit support — needs emotional registration beat

evidence.revised: Revised L211: 'She filed it. Later. But something in her chest shifted at the implication — the Seep had not abandoned her to the contract. The institution she had served, the one she had been ready to walk away from, had left her a way out woven into the very language of her chains. She did not have a name for what that meant. She filed that, too.'
evidence.assessment: Emotional registration added while maintaining the filing-for-later instinct. 'She did not have a name for what that meant' is perfect Lirien — classify-then-fail. 'She filed that, too' preserves the deferred-processing pattern the developmental editor wanted to keep.

Addressed by DEV-201 fix as both the finding itself and the revision log stated. The expanded aftermath passage (L237-241) naturally delays the mission redirect from ~4 lines post-proof-of-love to ~14 lines. Verified.

evidence.original: Draft L26: 'leaning into the anticipation like a predator scenting the end of a hunt'
evidence.revised: Revised L57: 'narrowing toward completion the way a contract narrows toward its final clause'
evidence.assessment: Stock thriller simile replaced with contractual-domain imagery. Character-appropriate for Lirien.

evidence.original: Draft L46: 'the realest thing in the room'
evidence.revised: Revised L77: 'the truest thing in the room'
evidence.assessment: Colloquial register corrected.

evidence.original: Draft L52: 'The sensation in her chest was terror, and it had Kaelith's name in it.'
evidence.revised: Revised L83: 'Noted: the sensation in her chest defied classification. It had Kaelith's name in it, and no framework around it.'
evidence.assessment: Direct label 'terror' eliminated. Classify-then-fail pattern ('defied classification') is more characteristic of Lirien. Field-note opener ('Noted:') reinforces operative voice naturally.

evidence.original: Draft L116: 'something older, something that belonged to the fae substrate'
evidence.revised: Revised L147: 'the raw resonance of the fae substrate where all binding magic was rooted'
evidence.assessment: Vague 'something older' replaced with specific world term 'raw resonance.' Direct naming instead of gesturing.

evidence.original: Draft L150: 'every crack, every channel, every route back to rhythm'
evidence.revised: Revised L181: 'every route back to rhythm'
evidence.assessment: Collapsed to single 'every.' Seep motto echo preserved with less dilution.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: 'tasted of iron and salt' stock phrasing

evidence.original: Draft L154: 'tasted of iron and salt'
evidence.revised: Revised L185: 'carrying the aftertaste of dissolved binding'
evidence.assessment: Stock fantasy descriptor replaced with world-specific magical reference.

evidence.original: Draft L186: 'the woman who built walls to survive and opened doors to love'
evidence.revised: Revised L217: 'the woman who kept people out until she chose not to'
evidence.assessment: Greeting-card walls/doors metaphor eliminated. Plain, direct language. Stronger.

evidence.original: Draft L200: 'Just skin against skin. Warmth meeting warmth.'
evidence.revised: Revised L231: 'Just her palm against the back of Kaelith's hand. Warmth without static.'
evidence.assessment: Generic romance construction replaced with specific physical detail ('palm against back of hand') and thematic callback ('without static' — binding interference absent).

evidence.original: Draft L30: 4 lines of uninterrupted technical dialogue
evidence.revised: Revised L61: 'her fingers found the edge of the table; held' — action beat inserted mid-briefing between 'thresholds for' and 'cardiac monitoring'
evidence.assessment: Physical gesture grounds clinical dialogue in the body. Creates tension between measured words and unclinical hands.

evidence.original: Draft L154: 'a fierce, stunned gratitude' + Draft L184: 'a fierce and fragile tenderness'
evidence.revised: Revised L185: 'a gratitude so sharp it hurt' + Revised L215: 'a fierce and fragile tenderness'
evidence.assessment: L154's 'fierce' replaced with 'so sharp it hurt' — different intensifier, no echo. L184's 'fierce and fragile' retained as the stronger construction.

evidence.original: Draft L160: 'Her right hand found Lirien's where it pressed against her sternum' (Lirien's LEFT hand per L86) then L170: 'Her right hand gripped in Kaelith's' (hand switched)
evidence.revised: Revised L191: 'Her right hand found Lirien's right — the free one, the one not pressed against her chest' then L201: 'Her right hand gripped in Kaelith's'
evidence.assessment: Now internally consistent. L117: left palm on sternum. L191: Kaelith grips Lirien's right (free) hand. L201: Lirien's left on chest, right in Kaelith's grip. All three references track correctly.

evidence.revised: Revised L247: 'A day's march, maybe two.'
evidence.assessment: Corrected to match continuity tracker (ch20 Day ~31-32, ch21 Day ~32-33 = 1-2 days gap).

evidence.original: Draft L60: 'Three hours of sustained contact'
evidence.revised: Revised L91: 'Three hours, closer to four, of sustained contact'
evidence.assessment: Corrected to match established '3-4 hours' duration from continuity tracker ch11.

evidence.original: Draft L92: 'electrical network', L100: 'electrical signal', L150: 'electrical cascade'
evidence.revised: Revised L123: 'conduction network', L131: 'The signal' (word 'electrical' dropped entirely), L181: 'conduction cascade'
evidence.grepVerification: grep for 'electrical' in revised returns zero matches.
evidence.assessment: All 3 instances replaced. 'Conduction' maintains anatomical precision without modern/scientific register. Consistent with fae knowledge systems.

findingsDeferred.title: British vs American English convention
findingsDeferred.deferralRationale: Deferred as non-issue. Manuscript uses British English throughout (verified against ch19 revised). Chapter 20's British English spellings (centre, catalogued, recognised, sensitised, channelling, unravelling, fulfil) are consistent with the manuscript-wide convention.
findingsDeferred.auditorAssessment: ACCEPTED. British English is the manuscript standard. No changes needed.

voiceMarkerSaturation.detail: Water-domain markers reduced from ~25 to ~12. Classification markers reduced from 27 to 19 grep-matching lines. No scene has per-paragraph marker saturation. The preparation scene retains highest classification density but this is contextually appropriate. No new voice markers were introduced by the revisions. The new aftermath passage (L237-241) adds 'catalogue' (L239) — one new classification marker in 5 lines of new text, within acceptable density.

dialogueNaturalism.detail: All dialogue retains natural register. Kaelith's lines remain clipped and military ('A day's march, maybe two' at L247). Lirien's briefing now grounded with physical action beat (L61). No dialogue was overloaded with voice markers or character tics.

continuity.detail: Hand tracking corrected (L191/L201). Timeline estimate corrected (L247). God-seal terminology corrected (L57). Oathbrand duration corrected (L91). No new factual errors introduced.

pacing.detail: Three-scene structure intact. Aftermath expansion (L237-241) flows naturally and provides earned breathing room. No dead spots introduced. Environmental transition retained at correct position.

showDontTell.detail: Four emotion-label clusters converted to sensation-based showing. No new tell violations introduced by the revisions. The new material in the aftermath uses physical description and internal monologue rather than emotion labels.

voiceConsistency.detail: Lirien's voice remains recognisable throughout: classification-first emotional processing, field-note fragments, analytical framework that breaks under pressure. New material at L237-241 is in voice ('Not analysing. Not measuring.' — though this introduces a new anaphoric instance, the content is voice-appropriate). Kaelith's dialogue voice unchanged.

darlingsSurvived.detail: All editor-flagged passages were addressed: (1) Purple prose at draft L132 — trimmed. (2) 'Skin against skin' at draft L200 — replaced with specific detail. (3) 'Built walls/opened doors' at draft L186 — replaced with plain language. (4) 'Most beautiful thing' at draft L158 — cut entirely. (5) 'Taste like ash' at draft L58 — replaced. No darlings survived revision unchanged.

traceabilityAssessment.details: 26 findings addressed with cited finding IDs in frontmatter and revision log. 1 finding deferred with documented rationale. All changes traceable to specific finding IDs. No orphaned changes (changes without attribution) detected.
traceabilityAssessment.observation: OBS-001: LINE-006 is listed as addressed in both frontmatter and revision log, and the log describes specific restructuring of L186. However, comparison of draft L186 with revised L217 shows the 'Not because... Not because...' anaphoric construction is preserved verbatim — only 'sensitised' changed to 'tuned' (which is attributable to COPY-006's anachronism fix, not LINE-006's structural fix). The revision log's claim for this specific instance is not supported by the text. This is a minor traceability gap, not a failure — the overall LINE-006 fix addressed 3 of 6 instances and retained 2 intentionally.

warnConditions.majorPartiallyAddressedDetail: LINE-006: 4 anaphoric instances remain vs. claimed 2 (1 unfixed at L217, 1 new at L237). The 3 successful restructures and 2 intentional retentions demonstrate meaningful improvement, but the claim of reducing to 2 is not met.
warnConditions.newRepetitionPatternDetail: WARN-001: 'In the bond, [subject] felt...' appears 4 times in 30 lines of the aftermath scene (L225, L229, L241, L255). This is a new formulaic construction introduced while eliminating 'through the bond.' The letter of LINE-003 is satisfied (exact phrase eliminated) but the spirit is partially undermined.

passFailGateChecklist.assessment: Zero automatic fail triggers. 2 warn conditions (below 3-warn failure threshold). Gate passes with warnings.

observations.detail: The 'In the bond' construction (5× in revised vs. 0× in draft) warrants monitoring in the beta reading pass. If beta readers flag it as repetitive, the chapter reviser should vary 2-3 instances to 'the bond showed,' 'she sensed through the resonance,' or implicit access where context is sufficient.

The aftermath expansion (L237-241) is excellent material that significantly improves the chapter. However, it introduces one new 'Not X. Not Y.' pattern ('Not analysing. Not measuring. Just resting.') that contributes to the LINE-006 warn. Future revisions should be mindful of introducing patterns that the same revision is reducing.

Classification vocabulary reduction, while passing the audit, shows a smaller percentage drop by grep lines (30%) than the revision log claims by token count (44%). The discrepancy arises because the revision log counted individual token instances while grep counts lines containing at least one match. Both metrics show meaningful reduction, but the token-level claim should be taken as approximate rather than precise.
